programme.title: Underworld
programme.desc: Vampires and werewolves have waged a nocturnal war against each other for centuries. But all bets are off when a female vampire warrior named Selene, who's famous for her strength and werewolf-hunting prowess, becomes smitten with a peace-loving male werewolf, Michael, who wants to end the war.

programme.title: Resident Evil
programme.desc: When a virus leaks from a top-secret facility, turning all resident researchers into ravenous zombies and their lab animals into mutated hounds from hell, the government sends in an elite military task force to contain the outbreak.

programme.title: Terminator 2: Judgment Day
programme.desc: Ten years after the events of the original, a reprogrammed T-800 is sent back in time to protect young John Connor from the shape-shifting T-1000. Together with his mother Sarah, he fights to stop Skynet from triggering a nuclear apocalypse.

programme.title: Every Which Way But Loose
programme.desc: Philo Beddoe is your regular, easygoing, truck-driving guy. He's also the best bar-room brawler west of the Rockies. And he lives with a 165-pound orangutan named Clyde. Like other guys, Philo finally falls in love - with a flighty singer who leads him on a screwball chase across the American Southwest. Nothing's in the way except a motorcycle gang, some cops, and legendary brawler Tank Murdock.

programme.title: Clue
programme.desc: Clue finds six colorful dinner guests gathered at the mansion of their host, Mr. Boddy -- who turns up dead after his secret is exposed: He was blackmailing all of them. With the killer among them, the guests and Boddy's chatty butler must suss out the culprit before the body count rises.

programme.title: Snowpiercer
programme.desc: In a future where a failed global-warming experiment kills off most life on the planet, a class system evolves aboard the Snowpiercer; a train that travels around the globe via a perpetual-motion engine.

programme.title: Predator
programme.desc: A team of commandos on a mission in a Central American jungle find themselves hunted by an extraterrestrial warrior.

programme.title: John Wick: Chapter 3 - Parabellum
programme.desc: In this third installment of the adrenaline-fueled action franchise, skilled assassin John Wick (Keanu Reeves) returns with a $14 million price tag on his head and an army of bounty-hunting killers on his trail. After killing a member of the shadowy international assassin's guild, the High Table, John Wick is excommunicado, but the world's most ruthless hit men and women await his every turn.

programme.title: Pitch Black
programme.desc: A transport ship crashes and leaves its crew stranded on a desert planet inhabited by bloodthirsty creatures that come out during an eclipse.

programme.title: The League of Extraordinary Gentlemen
programme.desc: Renowned adventurer Allan Quatermain leads a team of extraordinary figures with legendary powers to battle the technological terror of a madman known as "The Fantom". This "League" comprises seafarer and inventor Captain Nemo, vampire Mina Harker, an invisible man named Rodney Skinner, American Secret Service Agent Tom Sawyer, the ageless and invincible Dorian Gray, and the dangerous split personality of Dr. Jekyll and Mr. Hyde.

programme.title: Spider-Man
programme.desc: After being bitten by a genetically-modified spider, a shy teenager gains spider-like abilities that he uses to fight injustice as a masked superhero and face a vengeful enemy.

programme.title: Venom
programme.desc: After a faulty interview with the Life Foundation ruins his career, former reporter Eddie Brock's life is in pieces. Six months later, he comes across the Life Foundation again, and he comes into contact with an alien symbiote and becomes Venom, a parasitic antihero.

programme.title: John Wick
programme.desc: John Wick is a former hitman grieving the loss of his true love. When his home is broken into, robbed, and his dog killed, he is forced to return to action to exact revenge.

programme.title: Spaceballs
programme.desc: When the nefarious Dark Helmet hatches a plan to snatch Princess Vespa and steal her planet's air, space-bum-for-hire Lone Starr and his clueless sidekick fly to the rescue. Along the way, they meet Yogurt, who puts Lone Starr wise to the power of "The Schwartz." Can he master it in time to save the day?

programme.title: Beverly Hills Cop
programme.desc: Fast-talking, quick-thinking Detroit street cop Axel Foley has bent more than a few rules and regs in his time, but when his best friend is murdered, he heads to sunny Beverly Hills to work the case like only he can.

programme.title: Friday After Next
programme.desc: Craig and Day Day have finally moved out of their parents houses and into their own crib. The cousins work nights at a local mall as security guards. When their house is robbed on Christmas Eve they team up to track him down.

programme.title: The Naked Gun: From the Files of Police Squad!
programme.desc: A rerun of many of the gags from the television series Police Squad! (1982). An Airplane! (1980)-type spoof, this time with the an incompetent Lieutenant Frank Drebin, who always "gets his man". Visual gags come thick and fast, and it's impossible to catch them all with one viewing. The plot: Queen Elizabeth II of England is coming to town, and Vincent Ludwig has plans to assassinate her using a brainwashed baseball player.

programme.title: Police Academy 3: Back in Training
programme.desc: After it's announced that one of the two police academies in the state will be closed down, Lassard's alumni return to the school to train new recruits and compete against the other academy.

programme.title: Naked Gun 33 1/3: The Final Insult
programme.desc: Frank Drebin is persuaded out of retirement to go undercover in a state prison. There he has to find out what top terrorist, Rocco, has planned for when he escapes. Adding to his problems, Frank's wife, Jane, is desperate for a baby.

programme.title: The Naked Gun 2½: The Smell of Fear
programme.desc: Lieutenant Frank Drebin discovers that his ex-girlfriend's new beau is involved in a plot to kidnap a scientist who advocates solar energy.

programme.title: Brewster's Millions
programme.desc: A minor-league baseball player must spend $30 million in 30 days in order to inherit $300 million. However, he's not allowed to own any assets, destroy the money, gamble it, give it to charity, or tell anyone about the deal.

programme.title: See No Evil, Hear No Evil
programme.desc: A man is murdered. Two men witness it. A blind man who hears the killer, and a deaf man who sees them. The police don't think they're credible witnesses, but the killers don't want to take any chances. The two men must now work together to save themselves and bring the killers to justice.

programme.title: Private Parts
programme.desc: Having always wanted to be a disc-jockey, Howard Stern works his way painfully from radio at his 1970's college to a Detroit station. It is with a move to Washington that he hits on an outrageous off-the-wall style that catches audience attention. Despite his on-air blue talk, at home he is a loving husband. He needs all the support he can get when he joins NBC in New York and comes up against a very different vision of radio.

programme.title: Police Academy 4: Citizens on Patrol
programme.desc: A new batch of recruits arrives at Police Academy, this time a group of civilian volunteers who have joined Commandant Lassard's new Citizens on Patrol program. Although the community relations project has strong governmental support, a disgusted Captain Harris is determined to see it fail.

programme.title: Trading Places
programme.desc: A snobbish Wall Street commodities broker and a wily street hustler find their positions reversed as part of a bet by two callous millionaire brothers who run a brokerage house.

programme.title: My Cousin Vinny
programme.desc: Two carefree pals traveling through rural Alabama on their way back to college are mistakenly arrested and charged with murder. Fortunately, one of them has a cousin who's a lawyer - Vincent Gambini, a former auto mechanic from Brooklyn who has just passed his bar exam after his sixth try. When he arrives with his leather-clad girlfriend to try his first case, it's a real shock - for him and the Deep South!

programme.title: Click
programme.desc: A married workaholic, Michael Newman doesn't have time for his wife and children, not if he's to impress his ungrateful boss and earn a well-deserved promotion. So when he meets Morty, a loopy sales clerk, he gets the answer to his prayers: a magical remote that allows him to bypass life's little distractions with increasingly hysterical results.

programme.title: Beavis and Butt-Head Do the Universe
programme.desc: After a "creative" judge sentences them to space camp, a black hole sends our adolescent heroes 24 years into the modern future where the duo misuse iPhones, embark on a quest to score, and become targets of the Deep State.

programme.title: Prince of Space
programme.desc: A Group of space men from the planet Krankor who resemble chickens are led by their leader, Phantom to invade Earth. But a strange superhero named Prince of Space (actually a bootblack in disguise) arrives to defeat the spacemen. Although the weapons of the aliens cannot harm the Prince of Space, the Phantom continues to fight, and many plot twists (including capturing a group of elderly scientists) ensue. Watch out for the Giant!

programme.title: Island of Terror
programme.desc: A small, isolated island community's terrorised from a horde of creeping, blobbish, tentacled monsters which liquefy and digest the bones from living creatures. The community struggles to fight back, against these horrors.

programme.title: Lady Frankenstein
programme.desc: Baron Frankenstein's daughter and his assistant/her lover continue his experiments in an attempt to rebuild his legacy after he is killed by his psychotic, murderous first monster.

programme.title: Night of the Living Dead
programme.desc: A ragtag group of Pennsylvanians barricade themselves in an old farmhouse to remain safe from a horde of flesh-eating ghouls that are ravaging the Northeast of the United States.

programme.title: Renfield
programme.desc: Renfield, Dracula's tortured henchman, is forced to capture prey for his master and do his every bidding. But now, after centuries of servitude, Renfield is ready to see if there is a life outside his boss's shadow.

programme.title: Son of Frankenstein
programme.desc: Returning to the ancestral castle long after the death of the monster, the son of Dr. Frankenstein meets a mad shepherd who is hiding the comatose creature. To clear the family name, he revives the creature and tries to rehabilitate him.

programme.title: Soylent Green
programme.desc: A nightmarish futuristic fantasy about the controlling power of big corporations and an innocent cop who stumbles on the truth.

programme.title: Terror Train
programme.desc: A college fraternity prank goes wrong and a student ends up in the mental asylum. Three years later, it's graduation time, and the members of the fraternity decide to have a costume party aboard a train trip to celebrate their graduation. Unknowingly to them, a killer has slipped aboard, killing them off one by one, disguised in the costumes of the victims.

programme.title: The Dead Don't Die
programme.desc: The focus is on the sleepy small town of Centerville where the dead become undead.

programme.title: The Ghost of Frankenstein
programme.desc: Ygor resurrects Frankenstein's monster and brings him to the original doctor's son Ludwig for help. Ludwig, obsessed with the idea of restoring the monster to full power, is unaware that his various associates all have different ideas about whose brain should be transplanted into the monster's skull.

programme.title: The Invisible Man
programme.desc: A scientist finds a way of becoming invisible, but in doing so, he becomes murderously insane.

programme.title: The Invisible Man Returns
programme.desc: The owner of a coal mining operation, falsely imprisoned for fratricide, takes a drug to make him invisible, despite its side effect: gradual madness.

programme.title: The Invisible Man's Revenge
programme.desc: An eager scientist tests his new formula for invisibility on an escaped fugitive. When the formula works the criminal runs off to terrorize a family he believes cheated him out of a fortune years earlier.

programme.title: The Invisible Ray
programme.desc: Dr. Janos Rukh discovers a certain type of radium that has almost magical healing properties. But the element has a dangerous side, too, and it has already started affecting Rukh. Consumed by paranoia, he begins to suspect that his wife is having an affair. Wild for revenge, Rukh hatches a deadly plot...using his own poisoned body as a weapon to kill.

programme.title: The Invisible Woman
programme.desc: Eccentric Professor Gibbs, brilliant but impractical, invents an invisibility machine and advertises for a guinea pig. What he gets is Kitty Carroll, an attractive, adventurous model, who thinks being invisible would help her settle a few scores. Complications arise when three comic gangsters steal the machine to use on their boss. But they fail to reckon with the Revenge of the Invisible Woman!

programme.title: The Little Shop of Horrors
programme.desc: A clumsy young man working at an impoverished flower shop discovers that the strange plant he has been nurturing has an insatiable appetite for blood, forcing him to kill to feed it.

programme.title: The Monkey
programme.desc: When twin brothers Bill and Hal find their father's old monkey toy in the attic, a series of gruesome deaths start. The siblings decide to throw the toy away and move on with their lives, growing apart over the years.

programme.title: The Monolith Monsters
programme.desc: Rocks from a meteor which grow when in contact with water threaten a sleepy Southwestern desert community.

programme.title: The Mummy
programme.desc: An ancient Egyptian priest named Imhotep is revived when a British archaeological expedition finds his mummy and one of the researchers accidentally reads an ancient life-giving spell. Imhotep escapes from the field site and searches for the reincarnation of the soul of his lover.

programme.title: G Love and Special Sauce - Woodstock 99 Act 03
Don Kirshner's Rock Concert - Steppenwolf-Ufo-Bad Company-Foghat Live (1974)
Oingo Boingo Live At The Ritz (1985)
John Lennon and the plastic ono band Toronto 1969
Van Halen - Live Without a Net (1986)
World Championship Wrestling - 3x41 - World Championship Wrestling 126
World Championship Wrestling - 3x42 - World Championship Wrestling 127
World Championship Wrestling - 3x43 - World Championship Wrestling 128
World Championship Wrestling - 3x44 - World Championship Wrestling 129
World Championship Wrestling - 3x45 - World Championship Wrestling 130
World Championship Wrestling - 3x46 - World Championship Wrestling 131
World Championship Wrestling - 3x47 - World Championship Wrestling 132
World Championship Wrestling - 3x48 - World Championship Wrestling 133
World Championship Wrestling - 3x49 - World Championship Wrestling 134
World Championship Wrestling - 3x50 - World Championship Wrestling 135
World Championship Wrestling - 3x51 - World Championship Wrestling 136
World Championship Wrestling - 3x52 - World Championship Wrestling 137
World Championship Wrestling - 7x01 - World Championship Wrestling 294
World Championship Wrestling - 7x02 - World Championship Wrestling 295
World Championship Wrestling - 7x03 - World Championship Wrestling 296
World Championship Wrestling - 7x04 - World Championship Wrestling 297
World Championship Wrestling - 7x05 - World Championship Wrestling 298
World Championship Wrestling - 7x06 - World Championship Wrestling 299
Columbo
programme.desc: An old lady who writes popular mystery novels strongly suspects that her niece's husband murdered her, so she exacts revenge by asphyxiating him in her house safe while devising a solid alibi. It's up to Columbo to unravel the subterfuge.

programme.title: Maude
programme.desc: Walter and Arthur go away for the weekend on a camping trip. Maude is upset with Walter's plans but she soon forgets it, when Vivian and her go on a non-stop eating binge. However, Walter and Arthur's trip goes awry as they become victims of a speed trap and land in jail.

programme.title: Maude
programme.desc: Maude and Vivian go out for drinks one evening, while Arthur and Walter go away for a golfing weekend. The two girls soon compare notes and discover that an old flame who once dated both of them has sent them each a post card saying he'll be in town. The two girls quickly get drunk while debating over who he finds more attractive.

programme.title: The Golden Girls
programme.desc: When Sophia sets Dorothy up on a blind date, Dorothy secretly falls for the seemingly simple man, claiming "he's the greatest lover she ever had." Meanwhile, Blanche and Rose join the "Be a Pal" and become pals to two motherless girls.

programme.title: The Golden Girls
programme.desc: It's Valentine's Day, and the girls are all excited until each one of their dates cancel at the last minute. This leaves them to reminisce about Valentine's Day past.

programme.title: The Golden Girls
programme.desc: Dorothy wants to spend some "quality" time with Sophia so she takes Sophia away for the weekend to the Florida Keys. However, her attempts fail when a fed up Sophia storms out. Meanwhile, Rose and Blanche try their hand at writing children's stories.

programme.title: The Golden Palace
programme.desc: Spring Break finds a horde of college kids descending upon the hotel; Rose receives a visit from her granddaughter Charlene who is contemplating sex with her boyfriend.

programme.title: Sanford and Son
programme.sub-title: The Blind Mellow Jelly Collection (a.k.a.) The Chameleon
programme.desc: Lamont advises Fred to donate a collection of Blind Mellow Jelly records to a music library for a tax write-off. After doing so, Fred finds out from an appraiser that the records are valued at five hundred dollars. Fred, with dollar signs in his eyes, has Bubba act as though he were the son of Blind Mellow Jelly in an attempt to get them back.

programme.title: Sanford and Son
programme.sub-title: A House is Not a Poolroom
programme.desc: For a birthday present, Lamont buys Fred a pool table. But things get out of hand when Fred invites his buddies over for some friendly games. They quickly overstay their welcome and eat and drink Fred and Lamont out of house and home. Lamont quickly regrets ever buying the pool table for Fred and tries to convince him that the house is not a pool hall.

programme.title: All in the Family
programme.desc: The Bunkers head out to California for Christmas to find out that Mike and Gloria are having marital problems.

programme.title: All in the Family
programme.desc: With the entire family enjoying each other's company for Christmas in California, Gloria secretly confesses to Edith the real reason why they canceled their trip to New York. She and Mike have separated and are moving forward with a divorce.

programme.title: Supermarket Sweep
programme.sub-title: Sheralee & Kim vs. Brenda & Miriam vs. Michele & Kristy
programme.desc: Gourmet Week, Day 3

programme.title: Supermarket Sweep
programme.sub-title: Elizabeth & Paula vs. Pam & Kristen vs. Carrie & Scott
programme.desc: Gourmet Week, Day 4

programme.title: The Three Stooges
programme.desc: The stooges don't know it, but they are all engaged to the same girl, a gold-digger who plans to get an engagement ring from each of them and then abandon them. When all three show up at her house at the same time, a wild fight ensues, as each stooge accuses the others of making time with "his" girl. The stooges knock each other senseless and the girl escapes with their rings.

programme.title: The Three Stooges
programme.desc: The stooges graduate from dental school and go out west to open a practice. Everything goes well until Shemp "cures" an outlaw's toothache from the instructions in a carpentry book, and the boys must leave on the run.

programme.title: The Three Stooges
programme.desc: Set in the old west, the stooges become marshals in a town with a high death rate for lawmen. The boys set out prevent a marriage between the villain Blackie and the heroine Nell, who's father Blackie has kidnapped. The stooges manage to defeat Blackie and his henchmen, but when Nell's father learns she promised to marry Curly if he could save her, he decides death would be a preferable fate.

programme.title: The Three Stooges
programme.desc: The stooges appear in triplicate as three sets of triplets who were separated a long time ago. Their reunion causes confusion and troubles for various wives and sweethearts, but it all works out in the end.

programme.title: The Three Stooges
programme.desc: The Stooges are gold prospectors housed in the city dump. They are sent on a treasure hunt and dig through to a Federal Bank.

programme.title: The Three Stooges
programme.desc: The stooges are janitors working in the offices of Doctors Harts, Burns and Belcher. Some crooks arrive seeking medical attention after their boss has been wounded in a shoot out with the cops. Mistaken for doctors, the boys are forced to operate on the wounded crook, but instead they accidentally dump him out the window into a passing police car. The rest of the gang chases them into a store room filled with dummies where the cops finally catch the bad guys.

programme.title: The Three Stooges
programme.desc: The stooges witness an armed robbery and are brought in by the cops as suspects. After passing a lie detector test, the boys are freed and go back to their jobs in a Cafe. When one of the robbers comes into the Cafe, the boys recognize him and along with their friend Gladys trail him to a spooky house in the country where the crooks are hiding out. The bad guys abduct Gladys and the stooges must rescue her.

programme.title: Svengoolie
programme.desc: A bizarre series of sudden disappearances on the streets of New York City seems to point toward something unsavory living in the sewers.

programme.title: Roseanne
programme.desc: After Police Officer Jackie gets slightly injured, Gary says he's tired of always worrying about her and wants her to quit her job as a Police Woman. Meanwhile, Becky and Darlene find something disturbing under D.J.'s bed that makes them believe he's a complete psycho.

programme.title: Roseanne
programme.desc: Becky's folks pressure her to stop dating a boy.

programme.title: Roseanne
programme.desc: Roseanne, mistaken for a guy in her lumberjack Halloween costume, plays along with some braggarts and learns about machismo.

programme.title: Roseanne
programme.desc: Roseanne's PMS day and Dan's birthday are on a collision course.

programme.title: Roseanne
programme.desc: Becky is suspended for allegedly making an obscene gesture in her class photo which makes her suddenly popular at school, but Roseanne is the one called into the principal's office.

programme.title: Roseanne
programme.desc: Dan ill-advisedly tells Roseanne he's having dreams of a mysterious, and oddly homely, other woman.

programme.title: Roseanne
programme.sub-title: Do You Know Where Your Parents Are?
programme.desc: Since the girls continually break curfew, Dan and Roseanne celebrate the anniversary of their first lovemaking by revisiting the restaurant/hotel and staying out very, very late, hoping to teach the girls a lesson, with the help of Officer Jackie.

programme.title: Roseanne
programme.desc: An angry and deflated Roseanne discovered that her mother always thought sister Jackie had more potential.

programme.title: Are You Being Served?
programme.desc: Naughty sayings on her underwear push Mrs. Slocombe to the brink of giving her notice, but then it's discovered that Old Mr. Grace plans to ask a member of the staff to marry him. When he calls for Mrs. Slocombe, everyone assumes she's the one.

programme.title: Are You Being Served?
programme.desc: Grace Brothers hosts a week long sales event by selling only German goods. The staff, however, find that the German customers they are getting only want British goods. Captain Peacock objects to the "uniform" he's expected to wear and Mrs. Slocombe gets into the German wines.

programme.title: Toon in With Me
programme.desc: Bill and Toony watch cartoons that showcase major changes in animation.

programme.title: Columbo
programme.desc: One member of a mystery-writing-team decides to kill his more talented partner when the better writer decides to go solo.

programme.title: The Sopranos
programme.desc: Although Richie is miffed at Tony for forcing him to build a ramp for the pizza-parlor owner he paralyzed, he decides to make a peace offering. Unhappy with their lowly status as Christopher's lackeys, Sean and Matt decide to pledge their allegiance to Richie--through a violent, unexpected act.

programme.title: The Sopranos
programme.desc: Christopher has an out-of-body experience that spooks Paulie to no end. Carmela urges Tony to have a vasectomy. Visiting her own psychiatrist, Melfi reveals the 'unholy alliance' she's made with Tony...as well as her growing dependence on pills and alcohol.

programme.title: The Good Place
programme.desc: Jason visits with some people from his past while Chidi gets help in resolving a problem.

programme.title: The Good Place
programme.desc: Eleanor makes a startling discovery that tests her resolve, Tahani looks to make amends and Janet does some bonding.

programme.title: The Good Place
programme.sub-title: The Worst Possible Use of Free Will
programme.desc: Eleanor recalls some forgotten events from her past.

programme.title: House of the Dragon
programme.sub-title: The Red Dragon and the Gold
programme.desc: In Rhaenyra's absence and with no word from Daemon at Harrenhal, Rhaenys tries to keep the peace on the Black Council as Cole mounts a campaign into the Crownlands. In King's Landing, Aemond continues to undermine Aegon's fragile hold on authority.

programme.title: Game of Thrones
programme.desc: Bran trains with the Three-Eyed Raven. In King’s Landing, Jaime advises Tommen. Tyrion demands good news, but has to make his own. At Castle Black, the Night’s Watch stands behind Thorne. Ramsay Bolton proposes a plan, and Balon Greyjoy entertains other proposals.

programme.title: Game of Thrones
programme.desc: Daenerys arrives at Vaes Dothrak. Sam and Gilly sail for Horn Hill. Arya trains as No One. Varys finds information on the Sons of the Harpy. Ramsay receives a gift. Tommen meets with the High Sparrow. At Castle Black, a miracle occurs.

programme.title: House
programme.desc: A man awakens in New York after a bicycle accident unable to move or communicate in any way. House, himself injured in a motorcycle mishap, occupies the hospital bed next to Lee and quickly annoys the doctors treating them both by insisting that Lee has “locked-in” syndrome.

programme.title: House
programme.desc: Charlotte, an older woman who has spent the last six months taking care of her dying husband Eddie, is rushed to Princeton Plainsboro after collapsing from respiratory failure. The couple becomes a double mystery for the team when Eddie begins to improve as Charlotte’s condition worsens. The previously unthinkable becomes real when it seems that Charlotte will die before Eddie, and the team will be forced to make a difficult decision.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: After a stressful day, Marge has a nervous breakdown and parks her car in the middle of a bridge. Marge decides to take a vacation by herself. Bart and Lisa are sent to stay with Patty and Selma, while Homer is left at home to take care of Maggie. When Maggie goes missing, Homer only just manages to find her before Marge gets home.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: An anonymous letter writer makes ominous threats to Bart. The Simpsons discover they were sent by Sideshow Bob who, when released from prison, harasses the Simpsons and then attempts to kill Bart with a machete. But Bob's vanity foils him yet again.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Bart gets drunk during a St. Patrick's Day Parade, provoking the city to enforce a two-hundred year old prohibition law. Rex Banner, an Elliot Ness-type character, takes over the police force to help enforce the law since Wiggum has failed to do so. Meanwhile, Homer decides to make money for himself as a bootlegger, and is soon nicknamed "The Beer Baron".

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: When Marge leaves the house to recuperate from a skating injury, everything goes to hell - until Lisa convinces Homer and Bart they have leprosy.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Bart's class is on a field trip in the nuclear power plant in Springfield, where his father Homer works. When he breaks a pipe in the building, he is fired from his job, making him miserable. Homer is suddenly desperate for money and steals Bart's piggy bank for money, but all there is are a few cents. Homer feels guilty and he has the thought to threaten to kill himself. But Homer's family stops him and they tell him how much they love him.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: A social worker is assigned to make the Simpson family functional after they get arrested for fighting while playing Monopoly. He helps them learn how to work together and function as a family. The moment is ruined when, Ginger and Amber, the barmaids who married Homer and Flanders while they were drunk in "Viva Ned Flanders", arrive at the Simpsons' home, which outrages Marge.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Bart is punished by being barred from the Itchy and Scratchy movie.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Homer and Bart become father–son con artists (after Bart is given money out of pity when Homer decided to not take him home), but soon find that they are the ones being had.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Lottery fever hits Springfield, but the Simpsons are forced to forgo a lottery ticket, and many other luxuries, to pay for a life-saving operation for their dog, Santa's Little Helper. All their personal lives are affected negatively by the sacrifice, and they come to resent Santa's Little Helper. He runs away and is captured by Mr. Burns, who trains him to be a guard dog.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Bart creates a fake driver's license for himself and uses it to go on a spring break road trip with Milhouse, Nelson, and Martin. Meanwhile, Homer and Lisa bond after spending some quality time together at work.

programme.title: WWE Raw
programme.desc: With "Stone Cold" Steve Austin's spot in the main event at WrestleMania now secured, Mr. McMahon looks to make the show even bigger by revealing a special referee. The Rock challenges WWE Champion Mick Foley to a Ladder Match in one last effort to bring home the gold. Plus, X-Pac defends the European Championship and more.

programme.title: Schitt's Creek
programme.desc: Johnny and Moira have to deal with a surprise motel guest while Alexis and David go apartment hunting.

programme.title: Schitt's Creek
programme.desc: Moira’s campaign takes a turn when some of her lawn signs mysteriously disappear. David and Alexis must work together to get David’s boss out of legal trouble.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.sub-title: And The First Day Of School
programme.desc: Max attempts to make a good impression on her first day of pastry school, but finds the class clown to be extremely distracting – and intriguing. Meanwhile, Caroline dresses to impress Max’s handsome teacher, Nicolas.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.desc: Caroline attends her former nanny’s funeral, thinking the family will be honored to have her, but quickly finds out they have no idea who she is.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.desc: After Caroline has an incredible make-out session with Max's pastry school teacher, Chef Nicholas (recurring guest star GILLES MARINI), Caroline finds out something that makes her wish it never happened.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.desc: Max finds herself wanting to be more than just friends with her pastry school partner, Deke. Meanwhile, Caroline is "hot for teacher" - the married Chef Nicholas.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.desc: When Deke takes Max to his place after a great first date, his "home" is nothing like she expected. Meanwhile, Caroline feels empowered - then scared for her life - after having a shady car towed from the front of their apartment building.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.sub-title: And The Icing On The Cake
programme.desc: When Deke invites Max’s friends over for a gathering at his dumpster, Caroline learns an enormous secret about his background.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.desc: Max decides to break up with Deke now that she knows about his wealthy background.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.desc: Caroline asks Max and Deke to join her on her dinner date at Nicolas’s loft to help make sure she doesn’t do anything she might regret. Also, Max and Caroline treat themselves to very special hair appointments.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.desc: Caroline goes into a tailspin and brings Max into the mix when Nicolas tells her he's going to leave his wife for her.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.desc: Caroline tries to fit in with Max and the diner gang as they go out to get wild and crazy at the St. Patrick's Day parade, but finds herself longing to practice her former holiday traditions at the Plaza Hotel.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.desc: When Deke invites Max and Caroline to join him and his parents for dinner at their home, Max is tongue-tied until his mother confides in her about her secret past.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.sub-title: And The Wedding Cake Cake Cake
programme.desc: A girl who likes Max's cupcakes hires Max to make her wedding cake. Max who is still reeling from her break up, is hesitant to do it. She does it but the bride is constantly changing her mind about the cake and her marriage.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.sub-title: And The New Lease On Life
programme.desc: When Max and Caroline's rent controlled apartment's lease runs out they track down the original lease holder to stay with them for a day to renew the lease. But events turn south when the old man won't leave until he gets some action.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.desc: When Caroline and Max join Sophie at the racetrack as guests of Sophie's new bookie boyfriend, Caroline's reignited love for horse racing gets out of hand.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.desc: When Caroline discovers that Max didn’t graduate high school by failing to show up for only one exam, she decides to help her best friend get her degree – and reconnect with her estranged mother.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.desc: A reality show producer approaches Max and Caroline about shooting an episode of 'Keeping Up With The Kardashians' at their cupcake window.

programme.title: Ted Lasso
programme.desc: It's Christmas in Richmond. Rebecca enlists Ted for a secret mission, Roy and Keeley search for a miracle, and the Higginses open up their home.

programme.title: Ted Lasso
programme.desc: Nate learns how to be assertive from Keeley and Rebecca. Ted asks Roy for a favor.

programme.title: Ted Lasso
programme.desc: Ted is fired up that the new team dynamic seems to be working. But will they have a chance in the quarterfinal?

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.sub-title: And The Not Regular Down There
programme.desc: Max is both puzzled and curious when the new guy she is dating expresses hesitations about intimacy because he is "not regular down there." Also, Sophie and Oleg struggle to get pregnant.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.desc: When Caroline's grandmother Astrid awakens from a coma with no knowledge of her family's massive money scandal, Caroline attempts to keep the truth hidden for a bit longer by giving Astrid one last fancy luncheon.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.desc: Oleg's pro-basketball playing cousin comes to town to play a game against Max's favorite team and gifts her with two free tickets, but chaos ensues when the girls interfere with the game and risk ruining their cupcake business' reputation in the process.

programme.title: WWF Prime Time Wrestling
programme.desc: Gorilla Monsoon, Bobby "The Brain" Heenan and "Rowdy" Roddy Piper host exciting WWE action featuring The Ultimate Warrior, "The Million Dollar Man" Ted DiBiase and more. Jake "The Snake" Roberts appears on "The Brother Love Show."

programme.title: Bob's Burgers
programme.desc: Gene and Courtney get their big break when they are asked to be the new hosts of the morning announcements, but their romantic history threatens to get in the way. Meanwhile, Tina's attempt to play Cupid goes tragically awry when she volunteers to spear-head the Valentine's Day carnation fundraiser.

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Although Marie has always cooked the traditional turkey dinner for Thanksgiving, Debra decides it's her turn to prepare the holiday meal, but not wanting to compete with Marie's turkey, she chooses to serve fish.

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: When Ray's free time goes to coaching Nemo's basketball team in preparation for the Pizza League championships, he is reminded by Debra that their kids are growing up fast and he's missing precious time with them.

programme.title: The King of Queens
programme.desc: Doug's parents visit while Carrie's on a business trip and take over running the house, making Doug feel like a kid again. Also, Arthur tries to strike it rich by inventing a new type of screwdriver called "the Arthur's head."

programme.title: The King of Queens
programme.desc: When Carrie's new afterwork routine of apple martinis-along with an accompanying much more relaxed attitude-come to an abrupt end, Doug and Arthur join forces to keep her drunk, a course of action Lou doesn't approve of.

programme.title: Seinfeld
programme.desc: Kramer is annoyed with the catalogs he keeps getting in the mail. Elaine falls in love with a guy because of his smile. George's parents are avoiding him. Jerry can't find it in his heart to reject a gift from an old school friend.

programme.title: Seinfeld
programme.desc: Kramer turns his apartment into a talk-show set; Jerry's girlfriend won't let him play with her toys; George's clash with pigeons leads to problems with a squirrel; a new employee's silent movement rattles Elaine.

programme.title: Roseanne
programme.sub-title: The Courtship of Eddie, Dan's Father
programme.desc: Dan's visiting father astonishes everyone by announcing his plans to marry Crystal.

programme.title: Roseanne
programme.desc: Part 2 of 2. Roseanne throws Crystal a bridal shower and discovers yet another secret. Ed desperately tried to bond with Crystal's son Lonnie. Dan vehemently opposes this wedding, and is furious with Roseanne for participating in it against his wishes. Convinced that his father will ruin Crystal's life, Dan confronts the man in hopes of getting him to call the wedding off.

programme.title: The Office (US)
programme.desc: Michael was excited to know Dunder Mifflin bought television ad space in local markets, including Scranton. Michael wanted to create the perfect commercial. Only problem was Michael wasn't seeing eye to eye with guys who were going to be producing the commercial.

programme.title: The Office (US)
programme.desc: Karen was now the regional manager of the Utica branch and used her position of power to woo Stanley to her branch. For the right price, Stanley agreed to make the transfer. In Scranton the news was welcomed with applause, much to Michael's dismay.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: The Simpsons put the wild back in the wilderness when their RV goes over a cliff and Homer and Bart go for help, get lost and lose their clothes.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Homer and Bart are being pursued by an angry mob through the streets of Springfield, baying for their blood. It turns out that Bart, hoping to impress the town bullies, sawed the head of the statue of town founder Jedediah Springfield. But when he discovered how appalled they were to find the head gone, enlisted Homer's help in replacing it. After he apologizes to the crowd, they forgive him.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Homer's bowling-ball gift "for Marge" prompts her to take up the sport--and maybe also a handsome instructor who offers to teach her "many things."

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Shutterbug Bart captures Homer with a sexy entertainer at a bachelor party.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Bart flushes a cherry bomb at school, nearly injuring Principal Skinner's mother, Agnes, in the process. An exasperated Skinner tells Homer and Marge that since expulsion would not do Bart any good, he's placing him in a foreign-exchange student program. Bart is sent to France, where his host family are unethical winemakers who plan to employ him as a slave.

programme.title: Married... with Children
programme.desc: The rest of the Bundys endure sacrifices of constant loud noise, loss of living room space, as well as loss of electrical power as Al devotes himself to his lifelong dream: building his personal, exclusive, executive bathroom in the garage, with the centerpiece being a mighty Ferguson toilet for his flush. Meanwhile, Peggy seeks money to buy enough lottery tickets to win a multi-million dollar lottery.

programme.title: Married... with Children
programme.desc: To cheer up Peggy on her birthday, Al and Steve travel to "Francine's of Hollywood," lingerie shop in Wisconsin where lies the solution to Peggy's clothing crisis: a missing bra. Meanwhile, Marcy tries to cheer up Peggy in her way by hiring a male stripper.

programme.title: Married... with Children
programme.desc: Desperate Al and Steve put their heads together seeking solutions to impending baldness when Steve begins to notice his receding hairline, whereas Al doesn't care at all. After unsuccessfully trying some hair tonic for themselves, Steve tries joining a club for bald men called Bald American Dudes (B.A.D.) and brings Al along with him who holds their latest meeting at the shoe store.

programme.title: Married... with Children
programme.desc: When a psychic's predictions all come true for Al, Peggy and Steve, Marcy fears that what the psychic told her will come true - her death.

programme.title: Married... with Children
programme.desc: After the death of his longtime barber, Al lets his hair down rather than tolerate a visit to a stylist or a salon... staffed by women. When Al backs down and goes to a salon, he gets a really bad hairstyle that makes him look like a woman.

programme.title: Married... with Children
programme.desc: The Bundys inherit some money from a relative and decides to go to a fancy restaurant, but Al accidentally forgets his wallet. It is up to Bud and Kelly to go back and get the wallet, but they decide to ditch their parents at the restaurant, unable to pay the bill.

programme.title: Married... with Children
programme.desc: While Al teaches Bud about responsibility and the meaning of a dollar by taking him to the shoe store for the day, Peggy does the same, in her own inimitable style, to all that will listen to her during a Mother-Daughter Career Day at Kelly's high school, where she becomes the star attraction as the lazy, conniving housewife and role model to the other women there.

programme.title: Sanford and Son
programme.desc: The tax season has come and after an accountant goes through the books he finds that Fred and Lamont will end up owing the government. The accountant explains the situation and Lamont decides to go out and gets a job at a clothing store, so more money is coming into the household. Fred fears this may be the end of Sanford & Son Salvage.

programme.title: Sanford and Son
programme.desc: A Japanese land development company wants to buy out the neighborhood with plans to build a brewery in the area. Fred, holding out gets bitten by greed. Fred's greed is disguised as a "heartwarming" sentimentality and he's using it to squeeze as much money as he can get out of the developer.

programme.title: Sanford and Son
programme.sub-title: My Kingdom For a Horse (a.k.a.) First Night Out
programme.desc: Fred makes a deal and buys a horse with a great racing history. Fred tries to get the animal to breed so that he can have a lot of money making champions hoofing and stomping around the place. However, there is just one problem Fred must overcome, the horse he bought is a gelding, which has Lamont saying "I told you so" to yet another one of Fred's moneymaking schemes.

programme.title: Sanford and Son
programme.desc: Fred has a romantic night planned with Donna but Aunt Esther drops in at the last moment with some news, her neice, Elizabeth is in town and she wants Fred to have her stay with him. Fred is against the idea at first but when he takes one look at her he agrees, she is the spitting image of his late-wife, Elizabeth. However, there is another problem, Elizabeth is planning to move into an apartment in town and she will have two roommates, one female and the other male.

programme.title: Get a Life
programme.desc: There's a new waitress at the local diner, and Chris wants to take her out, but he needs to drive a car for the date. His problem is he never got a license.

programme.title: Get a Life
programme.desc: Chris becomes a house sitter in an old Victorian house that appears to be haunted.

programme.title: Looney Tunes
programme.desc: In this very abstract cartoon, a hard-of-hearing old Britisher finds a red horn and uses it as a megaphone, unaware that it is really a lost horn from the Devil's forehead. The Britisher finds that the horn has the effect of amplifying every sound psychedelically and causing him serious bodily harm

programme.title: Looney Tunes
programme.desc: Ralph Wolf and Sam Sheepdog are friends, housemates and coworkers who become bitter enemies, but strictly while they're on the clock. A suit of armor, a skin diving outfit, a unicycle and a makeshift tank figure in Ralph's schemes.

programme.title: Looney Tunes
programme.desc: In a spoof of TV's "Untouchables" Rocky and Mugsy chase "Elegant Ness" (Bugs) through the ACME cereal company.

programme.title: Looney Tunes
programme.desc: Bug's lands on Mars, claiming it in the name Earth; Marvin has other ideas.

programme.title: Looney Tunes
programme.desc: After getting lost on his vacation, Bugs ends up in the middle of Transylvania. He decides to get a room for the night in a nearby castle, which of course turns out to be home to a vampire. The bloodsucker is looking to make Bugs his next meal, but the bunny has a few tricks, as well as a few magic words, up his sleeve.

programme.title: Looney Tunes
programme.sub-title: To Beep or Not to Beep
programme.desc: Wile E. Coyote hopes to stop and catch the Road Runner using a huge, boulder-throwing catapult. But no matter where Wile E. positions himself, the catapult drops the boulder on him.

programme.title: Looney Tunes
programme.desc: A little boy tells the story of his dog, Bartholomew. One day, Bartholomew's tail was run over by the wheel of another boy's scooter. Consumed with rage, Bartholomew instantly detested wheels and tires and chased and bit into all wheels he could find, including the wheel of an airplane, which took him to the Sahara desert!

programme.title: Looney Tunes
programme.desc: Bugs bamboozles the Tasmanian Devil at the hospital.

programme.title: Looney Tunes
programme.desc: When Daffy hears that the Klondike trading post is paying good money for furs, Bugs' pelt becomes endangered.

programme.title: Looney Tunes
programme.desc: A Mexican retelling of the story of Cinderella.

programme.title: Beavis and Butt-Head
programme.desc: The boys incur the wrath of woman at the drive-in theatre, after taking a picture of her butt.

programme.title: Beavis and Butt-Head
programme.desc: Beavis and Butthead kill another Friday night at the Maxi Mart. They try to service the girls by working the cash register and the manager, whose in the back room watching the camera, thinks that he's being robbed and calls the police.

programme.title: Beavis and Butt-Head
programme.desc: The boys drive Principal McVicker insane, sending him to a mental home.

programme.title: Beavis and Butt-Head
programme.desc: Burger World starts a new delivery service; the boys make the first -- and last -- run.

programme.title: Beavis and Butt-Head
programme.desc:
A blackout forces Beavis and Butt-head out of the house to find a place to watch TV.
videos:
Radiohead - "Creep"
Morrissey - "November Spawned a Monster"

programme.title: Beavis and Butt-Head
programme.desc: Beavis and Butt-head plan to fake getting injured so they can get insurance money; real injuries follow.

programme.title: Beavis and Butt-Head
programme.desc: The boys go skeet shooting and Butt-head downs a jumbo jet.

programme.title: Mike Judge's Beavis and Butt-Head
programme.desc: Beavis and Butt-Head both want to wear the Burger World giant hamburger costume. Middle-aged Beavis and Butt-Head get a warehouse job at the world's biggest internet retail company. You know the one we mean.

programme.title: Mike Judge's Beavis and Butt-Head
programme.desc: Beavis and Butt-Head are invited to sleep over at Cody's house when his parents mistakenly think they're good friends.

programme.title: Beavis and Butt-Head
programme.desc: Beavis and Butt-head are almost recruited into the Army by Sergeant Dick Leaky, a.k.a. "The Rooster."

programme.title: Beavis and Butt-Head
programme.desc: After watching ""The Beverly Hillbillies"" on TV, Beavis and Butt-head decide to dig for oil but break a sewage line instead.

programme.title: Pee-wee's Playhouse
programme.desc: Today the secret word is "out," but Pee-wee is stuck in when he overdoes it and comes down with a cold! It's lovely Nurse Yvonne and ultra-health conscious Ricardo to the rescue with some good advice and chicken soup for their sick and cranky friend. In no time, Pee-wee's back to his old self again to join in the Playhouse fun.

programme.title: Pee-wee's Playhouse
programme.desc: Cowntess is having a birthday party and everyone else in the Playhouse has received an invitation - everyone, that is, except Pee-wee. First his feelings are hurt, then he gets mad. Just when he is about to give Cowntess a "piece of his mind" Reba shows up with his invitation.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.desc: When Earl is not invited to play with his old jazz band at their reunion show, Max and the diner gang join forces in order to get him back on stage performing.

programme.title: Mr. Wizard's World
programme.desc: - Light Sculpture - Paper Plate Illusion - Bacteria Sticker - Flash Fill - Ice Cubes Sticking - Childhood of Chimps - Word Processing - Pouring Along String

programme.title: Mr. Wizard's World
programme.desc: - Giant Bubbles - Water Strider Sex I.D. - Banana Surprise - Hero The Robot - Snake Tracker - Uphill Funnel - Muscle Pairs - Wing Bee - Bottle Pickup

programme.title: Beakman's World
programme.desc: Beakman discovers boomerangs, visits the soil, finds bacteria and the dung beetle then examines the mysteries of the circus.

programme.title: Beakman's World
programme.desc: Beakman tellls all about elephants, gets help from his mom in the Beakman family challenge and reveals the mystery of X-ray machines.

programme.title: Beakman's World
programme.desc: Beakman reveals the facts on skin, its functions and its reproduction, tells the truth about the "funny bone," and then explains why oxygen is so important.

programme.title: Beakman's World
programme.desc: Beakman reveals facts about bread, and Lester plays a housewife in a game show. Beakman then reveals the history and secrets behind measurements.

programme.title: Beakman's World
programme.desc: Beakman explains the differences between magnets and electromagnets. Art Burn uses gelatin to create jello. Phoebe needs the senses of the body explored.

programme.title: Bill Nye: The Science Guy
programme.desc: Before food gets into your kitchen, before it even gets to the store, it’s on a farm. Almost everything we eat is grown on a farm, an area of land used to raise animals and plants. Farming nowadays can get pretty complicated. Farmers are scientists – agricultural scientists. Farmers work hard to keep their farms healthy. Soil is stirred up to get oxygen to the microorganisms that live in between the grains and to plant roots – that’s called tilling the soil to aerate it. Plants need to be protected from pests with either chemical pesticides or biological pesticides. Animals like bats and friendly insects eat other pest insects (chomp,chomp). Animals are milked, corralled, fed, sheared, slaughtered or cleaned-up after.

programme.title: Bill Nye: The Science Guy
programme.desc: My, how you’ve grown! The popular exclamation from your Auntie may be no great revelation, but growing bigger is a part of life. In fact, it’s part of the whole cycle, or pattern, of human life that began with your birth. The different stages in life are called life cycles. Humans aren’t the only ones with life cycles. At first, you might think that you have nothing in common with a cactus in the desert, or a fish in the sea, or the mold in your gym shoe, but you do. You’re all alive. You all have or will experience birth, growth, reproduction, aging, and eventually, death.

programme.title: The Amazing World of Gumball
programme.desc: Gumball seeks justice when Banana Joe chews Darwin's pen.

programme.title: The Amazing World of Gumball
programme.desc: Darwin develops a texting addiction when he and Gumball get their first cell phone.

programme.title: The Amazing World of Gumball
programme.desc: Richard gets a job as a pizza delivery man, which worries Nicole when strange things begin to occur around the neighborhood.

programme.title: The Amazing World of Gumball
programme.desc: Gumball, Darwin and Anais go to a haunted-house party.

programme.title: The Amazing World of Gumball
programme.desc: Anais hunts for hidden treasure after uncovering a secret.

programme.title: The Amazing World of Gumball
programme.desc: Miss Simian owes Gumball an apology.

programme.title: The Amazing World of Gumball
programme.desc: Darwin tries to be more direct with Gumball as his teacher.

programme.title: The Amazing World of Gumball
programme.desc: The guys befriend a compulsive liar.

programme.title: The Amazing World of Gumball
programme.desc: Bobert becomes Gumball's servant and obeys every command he receives.

programme.title: The Amazing World of Gumball
programme.desc: Santa Claus is coming to town. Unfortunately he gets run over by the Wattersons, so it's up to them to save Christmas.

programme.title: Teen Titans Go!
programme.desc: Robin is determined to continue his streak of stopping 52 crimes during crime season.

programme.title: Teen Titans Go!
programme.sub-title: The Inner Beauty of a Cactus
programme.desc: The Titans play Spin The Bottle as Starfire tries to improve her vocabulary through kissing.

programme.title: Teen Titans Go!
programme.desc: The Titans have a hard time deciding who gets to choose which movie they watch on movie night.

programme.title: Teen Titans Go!
programme.desc: Beast Boy writes a song for Raven to express his feelings, then begins touring with the song, which upsets Raven.

programme.title: Teen Titans Go!
programme.desc: Beast Boy writes a song for Raven to express his feelings, then begins touring with the song, which upsets Raven.

programme.title: Teen Titans Go!
programme.desc: It's back to school for the Titans as they teach each other about science and grammar.

programme.title: Teen Titans Go!
programme.desc: The Titans are determined to find out what happened to the missing 2'oclock hour during Daylight Saving Time.

programme.title: Teen Titans Go!
programme.desc: It's St. Patrick's Day and after wishing to become a leprechaun, Beast Boy becomes obsessed with gold.

programme.title: The Ren & Stimpy Show
programme.desc: Ren goes into Stimpy's body to find a medical explanation to why he is so stupid.

programme.title: The Ren & Stimpy Show
programme.desc: Ren & Stimpy go to the Natural History Museum to see the skeletal remains of the mammoth Stimpysaurus. Wilbur Cobb tells them about the evolution of the great beast and says that even the meat-eating Renosaurus couldn't conquer the huge bumbling idiot.

programme.title: The Ren & Stimpy Show
programme.desc: Famished, Ren & Stimpy pretend to be long lost children of two Farmers.

programme.title: The Ren & Stimpy Show
programme.desc: Ren and Stimpy are two starving peasants in a fairy-tale world, so Stimpy tries to wrangle some wheels of magic singing cheese from the village idiot by besting him in a contest of stupidity.

programme.title: The Ren & Stimpy Show
programme.desc: Renwaldo sends Stimpleton on a food mission to trade their last chigger for some cheese. Stimpy meets a village idiot and trades his chigger for the magical golden singing cheeses.

programme.title: The Ren & Stimpy Show
programme.desc: In a deranged tribute to the "I Love Lucy" show, Stimpy lavishes affection on a store-bought oven-ready chicken. Next, Powdered-Toast Man and Waffle-Woman face off in a fight-to-the-death; the world hangs in the balance.

programme.title: The Ren & Stimpy Show
programme.sub-title: Powdered Toast Man vs. Waffle Woman
programme.desc: A woman tells the story of how her waffle business was ruined because of Powdered Toast. Meanwhile Powdered Toast Man tries to grant a wish for Little Jimmy.

programme.title: Beavis and Butt-Head
programme.desc:
"Buff-coat and Beaver" help Mr. Anderson build a swimming pool.
videos:
Sagat - "Why Is It? (Funk Dat)"
Ebn/Ozn - "AEIOU Sometimes Y"
Anthrax - "Hy Pro Glow"

programme.title: Beavis and Butt-Head
programme.desc:
Beavis & Butt-head visit a phony fortune teller who cons Beavis into thinking he has the gift of second sight.
videos:
Metallica - "For Whom the Bell Tolls"
Nina Hagen - "Herman Was His Name"
Donald Fagen - "Snowbound"

programme.title: Beavis and Butt-Head
programme.desc:
While taking change from a fountain at the mall the boys must run to a fake island in the center when the water is turned on,they remain and think of ways to get out without going into the water...which is only about a foot deep.
videos:
Whale - "Hobo Humpin' Slobo Babe"
The Cure - "Caterpillar"
Beck - "Pay No Mind"

programme.title: Beavis and Butt-Head
programme.desc:
The boys visit a drawing class (in hopes of getting free nachos) and see nude models.
videos:
Nine Inch Nails - "March Of The Pigs"
Dick Dale - "Nitro"
Superchunk - "Package Thief"

programme.title: Beavis and Butt-Head
programme.desc:
Beavis & Butt-head go to the movies where Lolita & Tanqueray trick them into waiting by the exit door to sneak them in for their date.
videos:
Bryan Adams, Sting & Rod Stewart - "All For Love"
Primus - "DMV"
Morbid Angel - "God of Emptiness"
Green Apple Quick Step - "Dirty Water Ocean"

programme.title: Double Dare
programme.sub-title: Moon Crickets (Loretta and Jose) vs. Firing Squad (Lisa and Jonathan)

programme.title: Double Dare
programme.sub-title: Prowling Panthers (Al & Yay) vs. Radical Warriors (Annette & Kevin)

programme.title: You Can't Do That on Television
programme.desc:
The Dukes of Hazard Dress Up in Drag will not be seen at this time so that we may present...
The rest of the cast is upset that Christine, as host, has an easier job. They trade places with her and all co-host the show, while she takes their positions in the sketches. In the end, she ends up on a quest to get Brodie slimed.

programme.title: You Can't Do That on Television
programme.desc:
Jaws Eats Jacques Cousteau will not be seen at this time so that we may present...
Christine hosts this episode from her bathtub at home. During the show she gets surprise visits from the cast and crew. The episode ends with her getting sucked down the drain.

programme.title: You Can't Do That on Television
programme.desc:
Atomic Blast Levels Fantasy Island will not be seen at this time so that we may present...
Jami admits that the only reason the Indians smoked was to kill the white settlers. He then tries to convince Christine to smoke.

programme.title: You Can't Do That on Television
programme.desc:
Mr. Rogers Vandalizes the Neighborhood will not be seen at this time so that we may present...
The entire set is stolen piece by piece, including the microphones and Christine's clothes.

programme.title: You Can't Do That on Television
programme.desc:
Mr. Rogers, Neighborhood Pusher will not be seen at this time so that we may present...
The cast uses pies as a substitute for pot to demonstrate the perils of drug use.

programme.title: You Can't Do That on Television
programme.desc:
Miss Piggy Eats Ham Sandwiches will not be seen at this time so that we may present...
Christine explains the importance of a healthy diet, but ends up wearing more of the food then she eats.

programme.title: You Can't Do That on Television
programme.desc:
Sixty Minutes will not be seen at this time so that we may present...
Lisa and Rodney keep trying to get Kevin to moon the cameras, but Christine stops them.

programme.title: You Can't Do That on Television
programme.desc:
The Incredible Hulk Runs out of Deodorant will not be seen at this time so that we may present...
Christine brings in a box of cosmetics and explains the use of each one. She offers advise about the dangers of using corn chip flavored lipstick.

programme.title: You Can't Do That on Television
programme.desc:
Pacman Eats General Hospital will not be seen at this time so that we may present...
Christine plays ATARI for the entire show, thus demonstrating how video games are addictive. In the sketches, many references are made to the soap General Hospital, and how addictive that is.

programme.title: MXC
programme.sub-title: The Amusement Park Industry vs. The World's Oldest Profession
programme.desc:
The employees of amusement parks take on those who give sex for hire. Also, Vic and Kenny have a go at a new game featuring deadly lasers, tanks, and some clown on a giant ball.
Games include: Hosin' the Ho's; Eat Shitake; Brass Balls; Sinkers and Floaters

programme.title: MXC
programme.desc:
Forensics experts take on kiddie entertainment folks as Vic and Kenny try to solve a murder on this special edition of "MXCSI".
Games include: Wall Bangers; Buck Off; Sinkers and Floaters; Brass Balls

programme.title: MXC
programme.desc:
Bush/Cheney supporters take on the Kerry/Edwards supporters in this special episode's battle between President Bush and Senator Kerry.
Games include: Sinkers and Floaters; Pie in the Sky; Boulder Dash; Character Assassination

programme.title: MXC
programme.desc:
People who strike oil take on people who give face lifts in this MXC competition.
Games include: Roulective Surgery; Rotating Surfboards of Death; Dash to Death; Mudd Butlers

programme.title: MXC
programme.desc:
Hollywood's film industry challenges a group with phobias on this edition of MXC. Also, Kenny teams up with Anne Heche to make a new movie, which Kenny treats like an adult movie.
Games include: Get a Piece; Saddle Sores; Pole Riders; Log Drop

programme.title: MXC
programme.desc:
The people who make love take on the people who own firearms. Make sure to keep your guns cocked!
Games include: Great Holes of Glory; Dope on a Rope; Skidmarkers; Wall Buggers

programme.title: MXC
programme.desc:
The footlockers take on the game boys (and girls) in this first episode of 2005. Kenny and Vic also try out some new athletic footwear.
Games include: Spare Me; Sinkers and Floaters; Chum in the Mouth; Boulder Dash

programme.title: MXC
programme.desc:
It's the septic sluggers vs. TV pluggers as waste management takes on advertising. Vic and Kenny discuss the significant role of advertising in American culture by exploring the ad campaign for Cocoa Puffs.
Games include: Bagel Bumpers; Sperm Wheelers; Tumbling Dominoes of Doom; Rotating Surfboard of Death

programme.title: Daria
programme.desc: The publisher of a "Savvy"-like teen magazine comes to Lawndale to hang out with Daria.

programme.title: Daria
programme.desc: Daria is forced to work side by side with Kevin at a mall nut stand.

programme.title: Daria
programme.desc: All the prodigal Lanes come home at once, forcing Jane to temporarily move in with Daria.

programme.title: Daria
programme.desc: Jake has a heart attack and his mother comes to the Morgendorffer household to take care of him; his heart attack inspires Quinn to study to become a heart doctor; two radio DJs broadcast their show outside Lawndale High.

programme.title: Daria
programme.desc: Daria and Quinn have to take a road trip to bail Jane and Trent out of a traffic violation.

programme.title: Daria
programme.desc: Secret service agents visit Lawndale High, and urge students to keep an eye of for students that might be different, and thus a threaten their safety, as well as the safety of the country. Though Daria & Jane become suspect numbers one and two, they actually start to notice an outbreak of weirdness among their fellow students, faculty members, and even relatives.

programme.title: Daria
programme.desc: The whole school goes on a mandatory-voluntary harbor cruise to raise money via casino gambling.

programme.title: Daria
programme.desc: Jane starts dating a guy, to the annoyance of her misanthropic friend Daria.

programme.title: Danger Mouse
programme.desc: Just when he's infiltrated Baron Greenbacks secret hideout, Dangermouse loses his memory after a crash on the head. GB takes advantage of the situation by making DM believe he is the second greatest criminal mastermind in the world, the White Shadow. In this new guise, DM immediately goes off on a relentless crime spree, stealing from the rich and giving to the poor.

programme.title: Danger Mouse
programme.desc: On his day of, Dangermouse decides to search for the long lost crown on Questzebottle (ancient King of the Aztecs and inventor of the practical joke) in the depths of the Amazonian Jungle. Baron Greenback decides to wait outside the temple and snatch the crown after DM has done all the difficult bits inside.

programme.title: Danger Mouse
programme.desc: A dragon has been terrorizing the countryside of Wales. Colonel K. sends Dangermouse and Penfold to investigate. Sure enough, they run into Jones the Dragon, who's small size belies his strength. Jones, for lack of a better 'damsel', kidnaps Penfold and takes him to his castle, a gift from his 'Frogfather', Baron Greenback.

programme.title: Danger Mouse
programme.desc: Greenback tests his new duplication machine on Penfold, which malfunctions and leaves him with hundreds of Penfolds to deal with.

programme.title: Danger Mouse
programme.desc: Colonel K. just barely manages to inform Dangermouse and Penfold that Greenback has established himself on the moon and is trying to jam all communication. So, our heroes pack their weightless boots and jump into the Spacehopper to head for the moon.

programme.title: Danger Mouse
programme.sub-title: The Next Ice Age Begins at Midnight
programme.desc: Greenback threatens to use his weather control machine to create an ice age if he is not crowned king of the world.

programme.title: Danger Mouse
programme.desc: When the United Nations inform Colonel K that aliens from another planet have announced their imminent arrival in Scotland, he sends his two top agents, Dangermouse and Penfold to meet them. Penfold describes the mechanical being they encounter as a 'clockwork mushroom'.
